document.name: Exception Logic – Step Therapy and Prior Authorization Exceptions

step_therapy_exceptions.description: Circumstances under which step therapy requirements may be waived for biologic therapies used to treat severe asthma.

exception_criteria.name: Documented intolerance or contraindication
exception_criteria.description: Member has documented intolerance or contraindication to required step therapy medications.
documentation_required: Physician attestation of intolerance or contraindication

exception_criteria.name: Severe adverse reaction history
exception_criteria.description: History of severe adverse reaction to controller therapies.
exception_criteria.severity_threshold: Anaphylaxis, severe bronchospasm, hospitalization due to drug reaction
documentation_required: Physician letter detailing event and ongoing risk

exception_criteria.name: Prior biologic therapy under another plan
exception_criteria.description: Prior biologic therapy initiated under another Medicare plan with documented clinical benefit.
documentation_required: Prior plan authorization records or pharmacy claims
Duration of prior biologic therapy (minimum 3 months)

exception_criteria.name: Continuity-of-care during coverage transition
exception_criteria.description: Continuity-of-care considerations during transition of coverage.
exception_criteria.applicability: New enrollment, plan transfer, or network change
documentation_required: Prior authorization from previous plan (if available)

step_therapy_exceptions.logic: ANY one exception criterion met is sufficient for waiver (OR)

prior_authorization_exceptions.description: Prior authorization is still required even when a step therapy exception is granted. However, certain PA processing exceptions may apply.

expedited_review_criteria.name: Urgent clinical need
expedited_review_criteria.description: Delay could seriously jeopardize the member's life, health, or ability to regain maximum function.
applies_to: Renewals when lapse in therapy would be harmful

expedited_review_criteria.name: Continuity-of-care urgent
expedited_review_criteria.description: Member transitioning coverage who will experience a gap in biologic therapy within 7 days.

decision_matrix.description: Combined decision logic for step therapy exception + prior authorization processing.

scenarios.action: Complete step therapy documentation required before PA review

scenarios.scenario: Exception granted (EXC-ST-1 or EXC-ST-2), standard request
scenarios.action: PA reviewed on medical necessity criteria only (skip step therapy check)

scenarios.scenario: Exception granted (EXC-ST-3 or EXC-ST-4), standard request
scenarios.pa_turnaround: 14 calendar days with medical director review
scenarios.action: PA reviewed with additional medical director validation
Expedited PA processing with clinical urgency documentation

scenarios.pa_turnaround: 72 hours if gap within 7 days, otherwise 14 calendar days

regulatory_references.cms_rule: CMS-0057-F (CMS Interoperability and Prior Authorization Final Rule)
requirements: Plans must include a specific reason for PA denials
Plans must report PA metrics including approval/denial rates and average turnaround times
Electronic prior authorization must be supported via FHIR-based APIs
Turnaround times must comply with Medicare Advantage regulatory standards
